entry.title: Re: Issue in Storage Location Determination in SAP ECC PP...
entry.content:
1. Pls check the BOM, whether maintain the storage location.

2. If the Storage Location in BOM is Blank.

You should check whether maintained the MRP Group for the material,

Then check the MRP Group configuration in background.

IMG Menu

Production-> Material Requirements Planning-> MRP Group-> Carry Out Overall Maintenance of MRP Groups

If you want to use the storage location in the production version, you can create a new MRP Group and set "Issue storage Location Selection" = "2". Then you can maintain the Finished material with this MRP Group, thank you.

 

Reference Link: Storage Location Determination in BOM Explosion | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Modification of accounting accounts for their allocat...
entry.content:
Hi Ana,

All material postings are determined based on configuration in Automatic Account Determination configuration. For Material accounts, the accounts are determined based on Plant/Valuation Area, Movement Type and Valuation Class.

Going by your example, it is the same material code- the valuation class is same, because we can maintain only one valuation class.

You want account determination based on nature of Service Order - Civil Engineering / Plumbing etc. If these two types of services are performed from different plans, you can use valuation grouping code along with Valuation Class in Automatic Account Determination, then you can assign different GL accounts.

If plant is same for different nature of service orders, then it is not possible to have different GL accounts. 

The other option is movement type. You can have different movement type for material issues for Civil Engineering, Plumbing etc. Link the movement type to new account key and hae separate GL account in Automatic Account Determination. But this will cause hardship on shop floor, because the shop floor should use right movement type based on nature of service order. Usually shop floor workers are not that intelligent. Not sure how many types of services you have. If you have more number of services, then so many movement types and GL accounts are required. This is not a best practice.

Material consumption is same whether it is used for Civil Engineering or Plumbing or other service. I suggest you have separate order type for Different nature of service and by using order type you can get the costs incurred for different services rather than using different consumption posting GL accounts. This is not a best practice.

entry.title: Re: Material Length - SAP Readiness Check
entry.content: Dear Customer, please understand that "SAP Readiness Check" helps only the Readiness Check report framework issues. so it has been removed from this questions. The BC-DWB-CEX-CCM is more appropriate to answer this question, however, I failed to find the tag in the community. Reporting the questions with correct tags help attract more attention from my colleagues to the question. Best regards, Vera Jiang

Robert

entry.title: URL OF QM
entry.content:
In S/4HC，T-CODE QA32 AND QM01 don't have url. According to previous OP experience, this function is required, thank you.

在S/4HC公有云中，QA32和QM01等事物代码，没有URL功能，根据以往OP经验是需要此功能，谢谢。

entry.title: Re: Filters in the Solution Manager scenario views (7.2 S...

Dear Kamal, 

You have created this content in Technology Q&A, which is a "question and answer" the forum.
Your content has been framed as a forum question instead of as content to consume.

This content is more appropriate for the Technology Blogs by Members page.
In the future, please post such blog content in that area instead of in the Q&A page.

Thank you and regards,
Vera Jiang

I am facing the same issue.
My devspace is not opening and is just stuck at Starting.
ID:ws-7h6zd
Please Help

Thanks

entry.title: Automation Alone Is Not a Strategy: Here’s Why
entry.content:
Automation Alone Is Not a Strategy: Here’s Why


Automation is revolutionizing industries, from finance to manufacturing, healthcare to retail. Companies worldwide are pouring millions into robotic process automation (RPA), AI-driven bots, and workflow automation. However, many businesses are seeing only marginal improvement  or, worse, outright failure in their automation projects.

The fundamental issue? Automation alone is not a strategy.

 Why Many Automation Projects Fail

Companies often approach automation with a narrow, tactical mindset—automating tasks instead of transforming processes. This limited view leads to inefficiencies, as automated workflows replicate existing broken processes rather than reimagining them.

For automation to deliver real value, it must be embedded in an end-to-end business transformation strategy, driven by data, intelligence, and a clear understanding of business outcomes. This is where SAP Business AI, SAP Joule, and SAP Build Process Automation come into play.

The Three Pillars of Strategic Automation

1. Align Automation to Business Outcomes
Instead of automating for efficiency alone, businesses must align automation initiatives with broader organizational goals:


✅ Customer experience: Automating customer support interactions using SAP Joule for AI-driven insights can improve response times and satisfaction levels.
✅ Revenue growth: Intelligent automation in SAP S/4HANAbcan optimize supply chains and reduce inefficiencies.
✅ Regulatory compliance: Automated auditing and reporting through SAP Business AI ensures compliance while reducing manual work.

2. End-to-End Process Transformation
Automation should not just replace human effort—it should enable new ways of working. This means:


🔹 Reevaluating workflows to remove redundant steps before automating.
🔹 Connecting enterprise applications using SAP Integration Suite to create seamless, intelligent workflows.
🔹 Leveraging AI-driven analytics to refine and optimize processes continuously.

3. AI-Driven Intelligence in Automation
The next generation of automation is intelligent automation, where AI and data analytics drive decision-making. With SAP Joule, enterprises can:


🧠 Predict issues before they arise and automate preventive actions.
🔍 Analyze vast amounts of business data from SAP Business Data Cloud to make automation smarter.
🤖 Create self-learning bots that adapt to changing business conditions.

Moving Beyond Tactical Automation

A truly strategic automation roadmap includes:


✅ Integration across enterprise applications
✅ Continuous optimization using AI insights
✅ A focus on business value, not just efficiency

Automation is a powerful tool, but without a strategy, it’s just optimization—not transformation.

How is your organization making automation more strategic? Let’s discuss!

entry.title: Solution Options for Product Management (PLM)
entry.content:
Summary

In this article we describe alternative options of an SAP application landscape that can provide Product Management capabilities according to the SAP Reference Architecture within the Products and Services enterprise domain (capabilities related to R&D and Engineering is discussed in a separate article). Within Product Management, we focus on the Product and Service Lifecycle Management (PLM) business area.
A list of criteria is intended to help evaluating the options and to make the right choice for a customer while taking their situation and preferences into account.
Please do not confuse the PLM business capabilities of the SAP Reference Architecture with SAP PLM solution portfolio where the applications are supporting both Product Management and R&D/Engineering capabilities.
  

Business Requirements

The business requires the ability to manage the definition, storage, organization and sharing of data generated during product and service development and throughout subsequent stages of the lifecycle.
According to SAP Reference Business Architecture, the Product and Service Lifecycle Management (PLM) business area comprises the following generic (cross-industry) capabilities (available to view in SAP Signavio Process Explorer or SAP LeanIX, under the Products & Services Enterprise Domain).

Business Capabilities

Business Process view

 

Solution Options

SAP’s product portfolio offers the following solutions which enable the Product Management capabilities (Product lifecycle management | SAP Help Portal

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Integrated Product Development
  • SAP 3D Visual Enterprise – Author, Generator, Viewer
  • SAP Teamcenter by Siemens
  • PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Engineering Control Center for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP product data management integration to SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Costing

In SAP centric landscapes S/4HANA core PLM capabilities continue to stay while customers can augment it with cloud-native SAP solutions and integrate it with leading third-party PLM platforms. However, moving forward the platform of innovations is the cloud-native SAP Integrated Product Development on BTP (SAP Business Technology Platform) with unified integration solution, which is specifically designed to foster a smooth and robust connection with leading third-party PLM platforms.

Characterization of the Solution Options

The brief description of the solutions below solely focuses on their PLM capabilities, it is not intended to detail the full scope of their functionality in this article.
For additional details please visit the SAP Supply Chain Management - PLM Portfolio Overview.

SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA contains all the standard core functionalities to support basic PLM foundation with mature capabilities to develop and industrialize products. SAP S/4HANA PLM remains a reliable system of record, inclusive of the direct CAD integration through SAP Engineering Control Center. SAP S/4HANA allows to manage product information holistically and efficiently across the complete lifecycle, ensuring end-to-end visibility from requirements to design to manufacturing to service, in both process and discrete industries.
The key capabilities of S/4HANA PLM are:

  • Product Change Management - Orchestrate product changes in a standardized, flexible, and compliant way
  • Authoring Tool Integration - Integrate mechanical, electrical, and electronic CAD authoring tools; synchronize metadata and structures
  • Design Systems Interoperability - Integrate external product lifecycle management tools and SAP; synchronize metadata and structures
  • Handover to Manufacturing - Deploy flexible manufacturing practices to initial product design, to improve quality, productivity, and agility.​
  • Model-Based Enterprise - Virtualize product models with drawings, manufacturing information (PMI) and geometric tolerances (GD&T).
  • Product Development - Control the development of complex products in a single, real-time environment. 
  • Recipe Development - Develop recipes and formulas rapidly, reusing existing ingredients, incl. packaging design and labeling.

For the full functional scope of S/4HANA PLM, please visit Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) | SAP Help Portal

Note that SAP S/4HANA solutions with “product lifecycle management” in their names will be changed to "integrated product development". For example, “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, product lifecycle management” will be renamed to “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, integrated product development".

 

SAP Integrated Product Development

SAP Integrated Product Development (IPD) is a comprehensive, cloud-native PLM suite running on the public cloud, leveraging the capabilities of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The adoption of a cloud infrastructure will enable integration of advanced Generative AI (GenAI) and native collaboration capabilities into the PLM suite, significantly enhancing the automation and collaboration aspects of the idea-to-market process.
(SAP IPD is a separate solution, not to confuse with SAP S/4HANA integrated product development - see naming change note above).

SAP’s Product Lifecycle Management cloud solution (formerly called SAP Enterprise Product Development) has been renamed to SAP Integrated Product Development as of January2025. This name change doesn’t impact the roadmap and the future direction of the product.
Core capabilities of the solution include

  • Innovation Management
  • Requirements & Systems Engineering
  • Product Data Management (Standard and Professional)
  • Product Data Integration (CAD Integration)
  • Product Data Exchange
  • Recipe Formulation
  • Product Handover
  • Product & Asset Visualization

More details on the product and roadmap:
SAP Integrated Product Development | SAP Help Portal
SAP Road Map Explorer

 

SAP 3D Visual Enterprise

SAP 3D Visual Enterprise Generator is a graphical processing client-server application that allows users to automate the processing of CAD and other 2D and 3D graphics. It can translate nearly any 3D CAD file into a lightweight format for downstream use. The solutions supports specific work processes in engineering, manufacturing, and maintenance. With SAP 3D Visual Enterprise Generator, you can create enterprise solutions that enable nontechnical users to search, access, manipulate, and integrate complex graphical data into any business or office application across an organization.
SAP 3D Visual Enterprise Author is a standalone application used to manage and author 2D, 3D, animation, video, and audio assets residing on a computer or network. The solution can create 3D animations and render rich visual content into common desktop documents, file formats, and business applications for secure downstream use.
SAP 3D Visual Enterprise Viewer provides visualization as a permanently available and fully integrated solution component. You can view and interact with authored and published content. This end-user tool is lightweight, powerful, easy to use – and free of charge. SAP 3D Visual Enterprise Viewer available on Android and iPad as well.
For details on the technical system landscape visit Technical System Landscape | SAP Help Portal

 

SAP Teamcenter by Siemens

Customers can leverage SAP Teamcenter to enable a comprehensive engineering focused PLM suite tailored for discrete manufacturing environments, especially with complex products (e.g. Aerospace & Defense, High Tech industry segments). For more details: SAP Teamcenter by Siemens | SAP Help Portal
Learn more about the partnership: SAP and Siemens – Better Together - SAP Community

 

PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA

SAP PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA (PLMSI) is an add-on that enables seamless integration of product-relevant data to realize bi-directional process integration between SAP S/4HANA and an external PLM system. PLM system integration replaces the SAP product data management integration (PDMI), the latter solutions mainstream maintenance ending by 12/31/2025.

PLMSI supports integration with

  • SAP Teamcenter by Siemens
  • Autodesk Vault
  • PTC Windchill
  • Dassault 3DEXPERIENCE

PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

 

SAP Engineering Control Center for SAP S/4HANA (ECTR)

SAP ECTR for SAP S/4HANA is a graphical user interface and a direct interface to the SAP system. The interface integrates the data and functions of heterogeneous applications in a uniform platform with SAP PLM. The graphical display of the high-level architecture is available in the Administrator Guide.
With SAP Engineering Control Center, you can integrate business data from SAP software with design information from authoring tools for mechanical computer-aided design (MCAD), electrical and electronic computer-aided design (ECAD), software and other simulation tools.

 

SAP Product Lifecycle Costing

SAP Product Lifecycle Costing (PLC) is a solution to calculate costs and other dimensions for new products or quotations in an early stage of the product lifecycle, to quickly identify cost drivers along the lifecycle, and to easily simulate and compare alternatives. PLC enables users to gain visibility across teams, proactively manage costs, and support new product innovations and customer-specific engineered products. You can develop product cost estimates at the early stages of product development, for customer quotations & throughout the product lifecycle. Connects to SAP ERP & S/4HANA, and legacy systems.
SAP PLC is an independent product (not an add-on) on SAP HANA – can be used as a standalone solution or integrated with SAP S/4HANA.
SAP Product Lifecycle Costing offers two system landscape options:

  • Minimal system landscape
  • Side-by-side scenario with SAP ERP

The minimal system landscape to run SAP Product Lifecycle Costing is to install the application standalone. This landscape is recommended when there is no need to consume data that from an SAP ERP system (e.g., existing master data).
In the side-by-side scenario you connect SAP Product Lifecycle Costing to an SAP ERP system to leverage and access existing master data and transactional data.

 

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

Industry specific requirements, product complexity and integration requirements are the main drivers for choosing the target solutions. We assume that SAP S/4HANA is part of the application architecture (current or target).
Consider the following criteria to define the right target architecture:

1. Industry sector

SAP PLM offers solutions both for discrete and process industry customers as well as asset intensive industries.
Process Industry (Formulated Products)
The well-established Recipe Management in SAP S/4HANA and the innovative features in Integrated Product Development (e.g. Specification Management and Recipe Formulation, Product Compliance check and Packaging and Delivery Mechanism Design) makes the solutions suitable for process industry companies (e.g., but not limited to Food & Beverage, Chemicals).
Industries like Life Sciences require GxP compliant solutions, e.g., S/4HANA, SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management (ICSM).
SAP Road Map for Integrated Product Development

Discrete Industry (Designed Products)
For customers in discrete industry segments, particularly in segments like Automotive Suppliers, Industrial Manufacturing, High-Tech and Consumer Products, SAP PLM offers a versatile approach tailored to unique customer scenarios and business processes. 

2. Product complexity

Especially in discrete industries, the target application portfolio is driven by the complexity of the products. 

  • Customers with low or moderate complexity: the go-to solution is the cloud-native SAP Integrated Product Development complementing the core ERP (S/4HANA), adept at meeting the specific needs of these customers. This approach is particularly suitable for customers requiring a straightforward, turnkey PLM solution. SAP IPD integrates with both SAP S/4HANA public and private editions.
  • Customers with advanced engineering centric requirements: customers in certain industries (e.g., High Tech and A&D) with high product complexity have the option to leverage third-party PLM application, like SAP Teamcenter by Siemens together with the next generation SAP PLM system integration solution. Alternatively, they can choose a hybrid approach based on the SAP PLM portfolio. 

3. Integration requirements

Customers with existing PLM system (non-SAP) and/or authoring (CAD) applications can leverage SAP solutions to integrate with SAP S/4HANA to create a seamless digital thread (systems & business process integration), establishing a single source of truth for product data from design to operate.

4. Object Visualization

Object visualization in PLM refers to the ability to visually represent digital product data, like 3D models and assemblies, within a PLM system, allowing users to interact with and analyze the design in a visual manner throughout the product lifecycle, from design to manufacturing and beyond; essentially, it's the process of creating a 3D visual representation of a product within the PLM platform to facilitate better understanding and collaboration across teams.
All major PLM platforms offer advanced visualization tools, therefor the SAP 3D Visual Enterprise is only an optional component.  SAP’s solution can be an option when advanced PLM application is not available. SAP Engineering Control Center (ECTR) can convert CAD to 3D Visual Enterprise.

SAP solution options based on industry characteristics and product complexity

 
* Companies with high product complexity and engineering requirements most likely have advanced investments in external PLM systems.

Conclusion

Companies using SAP have been running PLM in their ERP solution and by moving to SAP S/4HANA they can continue to do so.  While SAP S/4HANA provides the standard core PLM capabilities, customers recognize the need for more robust digital solution to manage complex PLM processes.
PLM is now increasingly moving towards the cloud, driven by the benefits that cloud computing offers. This shift is being catalyzed by the need for greater collaboration, automation, scalability and efficiency. 
By adopting the structured approach described above, you can position the SAP PLM solutions to effectively meet the diverse and complex needs of various industry segments, ensuring that each customer can leverage the right combination of solutions to optimize their product lifecycle management processes.

Table - BUT000
Field- BPKIND
in sap ABAP

entry.title: due date for credit memos FKK_CREATE_DOC
entry.content:
Hello,  I'm trying to use the function FKK_CREATE_DOC.

By credit memos is the due date in document for incommings paymens, not for outgoings...The FM don't accept credit terms for payment. Why? :):)

 

Thank you for help.

Regards Lenka

entry.title: In BDC Foundation service : Technical details
entry.content:
Hi Team,

what are the technology  & tools  used for Data Acquisition and Data transformation and Curation under Foundational services.

 

regards

G

entry.title: Re: Single Sign On Query
entry.content:
Hi Debadata,

 

in general, you can distinguish between two scenarios:

1. SAP Gui
2. Browser based access (Fiori, most Cloud Applications, WebDynpro, ...)

 

For SAP Gui, to my knowledge, only the SAP solution using the Secure Login Client is supported in RISE. There, you need the CommonCryptoLib as a cryptographic library in the backend. You can use Kerberos, local x.509 client certificates (incl. SmartCards) or shortlived x.509 certificates from the Secure Login Service. The latter one allows for scenarios like Multifactor Authentication as well as an integration in the customers own authentication infrastructure.

For the Browser, the recommendation (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-cloud-leading-authentication) is to integrate with Cloud Identity Service (SCI) either via SAML2 or OIDC (sometimes you have the choice, sometimes the application only supports one or the other) and then integrate there with the customer authentication infrastructure using either some form of local authentication at SCI with MFA or certificates or WebAuthn, or use a corporate IdP as a central point of authentication integrating there with SAML2 or OIDC.

 

These are the most common scenarios, and as well the ones I have seen working in a lot of customer environments.

 

Best regards,
Tobias

entry.title: Re: SD: Material XXX does not exist in warehouse XXX Mess...
entry.content:
The text for this standard error messave v1392 is "Material &1 does not exist in storage location &2 &3" and not warehouse as indicated by you.
Having said that, ensure, you have extended the material to the required storage location

entry.title: Sales order for a "Table", but deliveries for "surface" and table legs"
entry.content: Hi all, please advise a best solution for the case: Customer creates a Sales order for a table, but as a Supplier I deliver not a table but parts. So in the Sales order I have a material master "Table" and in deliveries I have "surface" , table legs" ets. how can I do it in S/4 private Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Single Sign On Query
entry.content: The CyberSafe TrustBroker products are also widely used on SAP RISE and approved for use on SAP RISE. So, SAP Secure Login Client is not the only product available. These products offer SSO and MFA for both SAP GUI and Web access to SAP RISE systems.

entry.title: Re: Hello, I have a dump in transaction MFBF
entry.content:
MFBF is a standard transaction and in that if system triggers some dump, it could be due to incorrect custom validation for that process.

Is the issue coming for all plants or only for a selected plant? Did you check the dump analysis via st22 where you have some tabs "Error analysis" and "Information on where it terminated"

entry.title: Re: Inventory audit report and trail balance amount miss...
entry.content:
Usually, the reason for this is one of the following:

  • there are manual postings to stock GL account (this might be manual JE, or simply manually choosing stock GL account on a document that does not affect stock value)
  • in account determination, stock GL account is used for posting something else other than stock value (for example, if stock GL account is set as expense account, price difference account or something similar)

entry.title: Re: Sales order for a "Table", but deliveries for "surfac...
entry.content: Explore Sales BOM functionality

 "error": "IAS authentication is not 'ias'. It is: xsuaa.

in btp cockpit security->users-> i found custom IAS tenent and checked all are ok but still getting the same error can any one help me to out.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements - Prerequisites SAPUI5 version

entry.title: Re: Filters in the Solution Manager scenario views (7.2 S...
entry.content: Thank you very much for the indication, I'll keep that in mind and modify my previous and future publications.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements - Prerequisites SAPUI5 version
entry.content:
I would also like to share my research results:

 

SAP UI5 Version

Feature

1.44

SAPUI5 OData V4 Model - SAP Fiori Elements that do not support the SAPUI5 OData V4 

1.50

SAPUI5 OData V4 Model

1.60

SAP Fiori Elements

1.61

SAPUI5 OData V4 Model – new Features – but not with Fiori Elements

1.80

SAP Fiori Elements - Features

1.82

SAP Fiori Elements - Features

1.85

SAPUI OData V4 (without restriction)

1.91

SAP Fiori Elements for OData V4

1.95

SAP Fiori Elements – Features for V2 und V4

1.102

SAP Fiori Elements – Features for V2 und V4

1.114

SAP Fiori Elements – Features for V4 – freeze first column

1.124

SAP Fiori Elements -  Quick View for link navigation

entry.title: Re: Missing left menu options in Integration Suite.
entry.content: Thank you for the response. I'll try this when I next find time to do the lesson.

entry.title: #CoE #DH01 How can we set different target GL for AFCV among companies in the same country?
entry.content:
Dear all,

We have several companies in singapore.
Company A want to exclude some of GL from Foreign currency valuation. Also Company B want to exclude some of GL from Foreign currency valuation in s4 hana public cloud.
However advanced Foreign currency valuation(AFCV) permit us to set target GL for AFCV not per company but per Accounting principal which means per country.
The problem is that Company A's GL for AFCV is not compatible with company B's GL for AFCV.

In such case, how can we set different target GL for AFCV among companies in the same country?
If there is any workaround in your knowledge, please let us know.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Triggering the SAP BPA bot when an email arrives via Outlook using VBA: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content: Thank you, that looks promising. But what are the steps on BPA side? @ShahIndrajit Thank you.

entry.title: Re: About the Coffee Corner – JOIN now, it’s interesting and fun!
entry.content:
Nice to meet you @sarah_niesel ! Your podcast sounds interesting. We've created a couple of podcasts recently in relation to SAP Community, events, and programs. I'm sure some of our community leaders would be interested.

Some tips for you to get connected:

  • Come and meet our current SAP Champions, some are new since January 2025 - such as @natallia_shtura and @Hilal_Kurt for example
  • You will surely be interested in our Blog It Forward campaign 2025 - introduce yourself to community leaders: @Sandra_Solis and @Raquel_Pereira_da_Cunha 
    @Lluis and @DanielMateo up next!!!

@StephanieMarley @AnnePetteroe @lauraschmitz @kimmecca

entry.title: Re: SAP SD IN FOCUS: WHY THE SALES AND DISTRIBUTION MODUL...
entry.content: Very good content. Would be beneficial if you post the content as a blog.

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Cloud Platform Transport Management service for SAP CPI Cloud Foundry environmen
entry.content:
Hi Narendar,

Yes, it' possible to create two trial global accounts and test the transport scenario of integration suite content.
Since a trial account is usually bound to a unique email, one trial account needs to be setup with e.g. your email, while the other account is setup with your colleague's email.

The first trial account then acts as the transport source together with cTMS and Integration Suite, while the second trial account serves as the transport target running another Integration Suite.

Best regards,

Sven

entry.title: Re: Delete Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is not possible
entry.content: Hello Jerry, I had the same idea, unfortunately I can't find the CDS view in the Custom CDS Views app when creating a CDS view. I have created a support Ticket because there is also no note or any community issue regarding this error message. I will write more information if I we get any solution. Thanks for your help. Best Regards Tim

entry.title: Re: Handling File Routing and Alerts in SAP CPI Based on File Type
entry.content: @harsh__chauhanI undestand that you want to send an email if non xml files are found but if you just want to get the XML files from the SFTP server, and leave the other files there with no modification, you can just use a simple filter adapter like *.xml or a more complex regular expression for matching specific patterns or upper/lower case letters in the file name

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Object Store activation in partner tenant
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

from Version 2025.07 the provisioning of the object store is available from "tenant configuration".

we have a partner tenant without the possibility to access to "tenant configuration" page.

How we can activate the object store for a partner tenant? ( i think it's a TDD environment) 

Thanks,
Alberto

entry.title: Re: sap basis coerce

entry.title: Re: Email Template with Table in Email Body (SAP_Basis release 750 or above and S/4)
entry.content:
Hello,

thank you for the Example. 
I´m using the template in some Reports as a Job. Sometimes, 1-2 times a month, i get following error message: 

 SAVE DOCUMENT TO DB KPRO_DOC

      TRY.
          DATA(li_email_api_ref) = cl_smtg_email_api=>get_instance( iv_template_id = lc_template_id ).
        CATCH cx_send_req_bcs INTO lx_send_req_bcs.
          MESSAGE lx_send_req_bcs TYPE 'E'.
        CATCH cx_smtg_email_common INTO lx_smtg_email_common.
          MESSAGE lx_smtg_email_common TYPE 'E'.
      ENDTRY.

The get_instance method is in a Loop. Could this be the reason for this error or someone experienced the same error?! Thanks for any ideas

entry.content:

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What is the GS1-128 barcode format

SAP told me the quantity is not support via GS1 barcode scanning because it may cause the quantity difference during picking process. We must enter it manually. Thanks for your help, regards Emmanuel

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠

entry.title: Remove SOAP envelope and namespace prefix using XSLT mapping in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Sapians,

Sometimes, we encountered an issue where CPI is getting data from SOAP sender channel which have SOAP envelope and namespace prefix in it and we face issue while processing that. Especially if we need to route the flow as per the condition. In that case, we need to read the value coming in SOAP fields and process further. The XML condition of router will not get processed until the SOAP envelope and namespace prefix are removed.

To resolve this issue, we can use XSLT mapping which can remove the entire SOAP envelope and namespace prefix to process further in SAP CPI.

Sample SOAP payload :

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope" soap:encodingStyle="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-encoding">
<soap:Body>
<m:GetPrice xmlns:m="https://www.w3schools.com/prices">
<m:Item>Apples</m:Item>
<m:Price>110</m:Price>
</m:GetPrice>
</soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

XSLT mapping to remove namespace and prefix.

<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
<xsl:output method="xml" omit-xml-declaration="yes" />
<xsl:template match="/|comment()|processing-instruction()">
<xsl:copy>
<xsl:apply-templates />
</xsl:copy>
</xsl:template>
<xsl:template match="*">
<xsl:element name="{local-name()}">
<xsl:apply-templates select="@*|node()" />
</xsl:element>
</xsl:template>
<xsl:template match="@*">
<xsl:attribute name="{local-name()}">
<xsl:value-of select="." />
</xsl:attribute>
</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

 

Output payload :

 

<Envelope encodingStyle="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-encoding">
    <Body>
        <GetPrice>
            <Item>Apples</Item>
            <Price>110</Price>
        </GetPrice>
    </Body>
</Envelope>
 

This conclude the blog😊, with this the SOAP envelope and namespace prefix will be removed in SAP CPI.

In case of any queries, please comment down. Will try to resolve your concerns😊.

entry.title: Re: INT4 doesn't accept negative value
entry.content:
I had the same problem with INT4. I did a cast to quan. That did the trick for me:

cast (DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN( coalesce( Versand.CreationDate, SDIA.CreationDate ), SDIA.CreationDate) as abap.quan(10, 0 ) )

entry.title: SAP Business Transformation Center: End to End Digital Blueprint creation
entry.content:
SAP Business Transformation Center: End to End Digital Blueprint creation

What is Business Transformation Center (BTC)?

Companies who wants to go with Bluefield conversion (Selective Data Transition) approach, SAP has given a new Tool to selectively migrate their Master and Transactional data for respective company codes only not for all. The new tool is Business Transformation Center (BTC).

 

BTC Tool and its process is only supported for Bluefields Projects (Selective Data Transition) not for Brownfield and Greenfield Projects. It can work for On premise and On Cloud (Public) solutions only.

 

In this blog I’ll show how to create Digital Blueprint in SAP BTC Tool “Company Code wise”.

How Business Transformation Center (BTC) tool works?

For  End to End and Technical configuration of BTC Tool, please refer the Blog publishes by my colleague.

SDT Using SAP Business Transformation Center: End ... - SAP Community

 

Company Code wise Data Migration.

BTC tool has 3 major phases:

  • Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition
  • Selective Data Transition: Model your Transformation and
  • Selective Data Transition: Execute and Monitor your Transformation

 

In this blog we will only cover “Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition”  Digital Blueprint creation part.

  • Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition

The first task in BTC Tool is to do the Scoping which can be done through “Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transformation app”.

 

The first two apps “Manage Analysis Files and Manage System Scan” are Basis activity.

Once the Analysis File is uploaded and system scanning is done next step is to create a Digital Blueprint. Digital Blueprint is a heart of BTC tool process, without Digital Blueprint scoping and the Selective Data Migration is not possible in S4 HANA system.

Before creation of Digital Blueprint, we need to have a detailed workshop with business on organization AS IS business process. After the ASI IS workshop with business is completed, we can then discuss with business on the Digital Blueprint scoping like what company codes  which company codes will be In scope and Out of Scope. Only the In-scope company codes data will be moved to S4 HANA system. The out-of-scope company codes will not be moved to S4 HANA system.

 

Digital Blueprint creation Process

We can create Digital Blueprint using “Manage Digital Blueprint App”

 

Click on Create and fill the necessary details.

 

 

 

 

The system will show us the list of all the company codes from source system.

 

 

 

 

Navigate to Company Code App and click on Mass Edit and choose the company codes to be in scope and out of scope

 

 

In my test case I took 4 company codes in Scope out of 80 and rest 76 are out of scope

AR01, HK01,TH01 and PH01

 

 

We can also see the In scope company code in ‘Mange Digital Blueprint” also. We can also choose Transformation Objects based on business requirement and can make the objects In scope or out of scope. 

We can navigate to Select Transformation app by selecting the Related Apps from Select Company Code app itself and click on Select Transformation Objects

 

 

 

System will show you all the Transformation objects in scope initially. In our case Total Transformation Objects were 169

We can discuss with business what Transformation objects to be kept in Scope or out of scope by referring last activity and Total Data Count.

We can exclude data whose Total Data Count= “0” or we can also check if last activity for that objects are very old.

As seen we have chosen all the 169 Transformation Objects in Scope for our 4 company codes which can be seen in the app

 

 

The Select Scanned table in “The Select Scanned Table in the  Selective Data Transition: Define the Scope of your Transition” app will list all the standard and custom tables in the Digital Blue Print

 

We can further navigate to Digital Blueprint overview app to get the graphical detail of the Company Code Selection, Transformation object Selection and so on.

 

 

 

The final step is to confirm the Digital blueprint when all the above scoping is done with business. This step is mandatory to move to the next step of Preparation and Execution of Data Migration

Note: Once Digital Blueprint is confirmed it cannot be changed further. For any new requirement new Digital Blueprint has to be created every time.

Hope this blog has helped you in understanding of how Digital Blueprint works in SAP BTC tool as a starting step for data migration to S4 HANA system.

cast (DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN( coalesce( Versand.CreationDate, SDIA.CreationDate ), SDIA.CreationDate) as abap.quan(10, 0 ) )

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP BO to change the content of a customizing table in the production client
entry.content:
@ShyamPrasad 

The generated objects (ABAP class, behavior definition, CDS view, ...) are always recorded on a workbench request and can therefore be transported to other clients.
"No Transport" only means that the generated application has no select transport action and does not record the changes regarding the content of the configuration table in a customizing request.
In addition, the adaptable in production setting is set to true so that you can change the configuration table in the test and production tenants in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
To change the adaptable in production setting, open the transport object definition in ADT:

entry.title: Standalone As-Built Configuration in SAP ME - Excess component Qty removal
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have stuck at a point while developing a custom solution for Excess qty removal for components in SFCs.

Issue: Due to changes in BOM in S4 after order release and start of execution in SAP ME, there is an error during the completion of the operation "Check config for <<SFC>> reports <<QTY>> missing <<component>>, 0 excess, 0 non-BOM and 0 test part Components (Message 13920)". upon checking, found that due to changes, there is an excess qty for the component is available, using standalone As-Built configuration activity, we were able to return the excess qty for an SFC but it's taking huge efforts to return for each SFC.

So, I have started developing a solution where the input will be the shop order and all the SFCs with the excess qty in the component should be returned.
I'm able to fetch the excess qty info from SFC_ASSY & SFC_ASSY_LOG tables.

I tried to use removeComponents PAPI but getting an error while executing the MII transaction.

can you please let me know if there is any standard service/solution is available to return the excess qty.

Thank You!

Hello @natalia_vilinskaya

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: When will Joule be released also in Italian language?
entry.content:
Hello, 

Currently there is no publically available date showing availability for Italian. This can change over time so I recommend checking the roadmap viewer closer to the next release. I include the link below. 

The languages that will currently be supported in the H1 2025 release are visible in this document. As you can see in the document there is a note stating that even if a language is not supported the AI can often still understand the question and will give a response in English.  See the guide below. 
https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/joule-capabilities/8d44120a69844638ad160569b8bdd60e.html?state=DRAFT&version=DEV

From the guide "Because Joule relies on a Large Language Model, Joule can understand languages other than those officially supported. If you send a request in a language other than those supported by Joule, it is possible that Joule will understand the request and provide a response. However, if Joule is able to understand the request, the response will be provided in the language set by the associated SAP application."

Roadmap Viewer showing Upcoming features and timelines -
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=CURRENT-LAST&PRODUCT=40F2E9281A631EDA94C7C01F9A1F40F4#Q2%202025 

If you feel there is an urgent need for this language to be supported you can submit an influence request.  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3768 

Paul

entry.title: Request for Reconsideration of Yearly Certification Renewal Policy – SAP ABAP on HANA (C_TAW12_750)
entry.content:
Greetings!

I am writing to express a concern shared by many senior SAP consultants, including myself, who specialize in ABAP on HANA and are planning for certification.

About Me:

  • SAP ABAP Consultant with over 9 years of hands-on project experience.
  • Proficient in Core ABAP, ABAP on HANA, ODATA, Adobe Forms, and SAP Script.
  • Currently preparing for the SAP Certified Development Associate - ABAP for SAP HANA 2.0 (Exam Code: C_TAW12_750).

Learning Path Completed:

  • TAW10e — ABAP Fundamentals
  • TAW12e — Advanced ABAP on HANA
  • HA100 — SAP HANA Introduction
  • HA400 — ABAP Programming for SAP HANA

My career goal is to continue working in ABAP & HANA for the next decade without shifting to other technologies like Fiori, RAP, or Cloud-only models.

Key Concern: Given my commitment to working in the same technology for the foreseeable future, I question the necessity of paying substantial fees annually for certification renewal or mandatory Learning Hub subscriptions.

Challenges Faced by Consultants:

  • Lack of reimbursement from most employers.
  • No salary increase or bonus tied to certification.
  • Absence of financial support from employers.

Arguments Against Yearly Renewal:

  1. ABAP is a mature and stable language.
  2. Core ABAP & ABAP on HANA do not undergo yearly changes like Cloud products.
  3. Why should we pay annually if we are working on the same release and technology?
  4. Renewal should be based on need, not time.
  5. The current renewal policy appears to be more of a revenue-generation model than a knowledge validation process.
  6. Consultants working in support or maintenance projects may not use new features.
  7. SAP does not offer any special benefits for paying yearly renewal fees.

Suggestions for SAP:

  • Introduce Lifetime Certification for stable SAP modules like ABAP.
  • Require renewal only when a major version change occurs (e.g., S/4HANA Cloud RAP mandatory knowledge).
  • Reduce Learning Hub costs for individual consultants.
  • Offer special Learning Hub pricing for self-learners or freelancers.
  • Recognize long-term consultants with experience-based recognition badges.

Final Question: Why should consultants pay yearly fees to validate their knowledge in a technology where no new learning or project exposure is required?

entry.title: COMING SOON - the place to connect & exchange on SAP Learning Hub, student edition
entry.content: This is a dedicated space for lecturers to share experiences, discuss best practices, and connect with peers and SAP experts. Here, you can exchange insights on how to integrate SAP Learning Hub, student edition into your curriculum and prepare students for career success with SAP skills.

entry.title: Re: Deactivating Simplified Sourcing in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for this interesting blog!

I just want to comment on the conclusion. as per the logic described in the examples shouldn't we  highlight the conclusion this way : 

  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will be automatically selected as a source of supply.
  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will only be selected as a source of supply if there is a source list entry determining that they should be selected by MRP.

Kr,

entry.title: Re: Sales order for a "Table", but deliveries for "surfac...
entry.content: Thanks a lot! really helps

entry.title: Re: Hello, I have a dump in transaction MFBF
entry.content:
Hello @David_mT 

It looks like you are using a batch determination quantity proposal procedure 600 which is not defined in your system. That is strange as procedure 600 is part of standard delivery:

************************************************************************
* FORM CHMVS_600
************************************************************************
* Routine to distribute the available quantities of batches.
* The distribution is an example for a TOP-DOWN distribution
* with regard to the sort sequence.
* To set up your own distribution rules, sort the following table
* according to your requirements.
*
* Structure of internal table DISQTY (significant fields):
* --------------------------------------------------------
*    DATA: BEGIN OF DISQTY OCCURS 0,
*            MATNR     LIKE BDBATCH-MATNR,    -> material number
*            CHARG     LIKE BDBATCH-CHARG,    -> batch number
*            WERKS     LIKE BDBATCH-WERKS,    -> plant
*            LGORT     LIKE BDBATCH-LGORT,    -> storage location
*            AVAL_QUAN LIKE V01FDK-VRFMG,     -> available quantity
*            QUANTITY  LIKE BDBATCH-MENGE,    -> target quantity
*            RES_TABIX LIKE SY-TABIX.         ->internal use
*    DATA: END OF DISQTY.
*
* Additional fields for distribution:
* -----------------------------------
* NO_OF_SPLIT -> The number of possible batch quantity splits
* QUAN_TO_DIS -> The quantity to be distributed (ordered quantity)
*
* Restrictions!
* -------------
* 1) Don't add or delete any entries from the internal table
* 2) Don't modify fields MATNR, CHARG, WERKS, LGORT, AVAL_QUAN and
*    RES_TABIX.
* 3) Don't change values of NO_OF_SPLIT and QUAN_TO_DIS
* 4) The only field that can be modified is the DISQTY-QUANTITY field
************************************************************************
FORM chmvs_600.

* BEGIN: get valuation type from PO line item
* the logic copied from the email. Can't verify it without access to Yara system.
* Assuming it is correct

  DATA: lv_delnr  TYPE lips-vbeln,
        lv_delpos TYPE lips-posnr,
        lv_ebeln  TYPE ekpo-ebeln,
        lv_ebelp  TYPE ekpo-ebelp.

...

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: VM status is Active for SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 & SAP...
entry.content:
Hi WritwikIERPA ,

 

According to our log files the main reason for the VM status is that you have requested more vCPU capacity than your current vCPU limit of 44 allows for the instance bucket that the specified instance type belongs to.

You could adjust your quota and then to try again. It could take some time for the changes to take effect. 

Please also note that the number of used by you vCPUs has been calculated as an sum off all vCPUs of your SAP Cloud Appliance Library appliances and also of these, started directly via the Cloud provider Console.

For more information, see our FAQ -> How to increase your AWS Service quotas? question.

 

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Re: Are the SF LMS course completion timestamp a trusted\...
entry.content:
Hi, 

I think you are asking two questions here. 

1- Can the completion status in the learning system be trusted as accurate - the answer here would be yes?

2- Can these completion status be used as to meet the requirements of of a specific EU Funding Program?
This is a question that can only be answered by you comparing the exact requirements of Funding program and what is acceptable to be submitted vs the information that the system can provide. 
So its something that should be checked with your company's legal department. 

You could also consider the E-Signature functionality which is what is used for some industries that require strict compliance requirements. However, as stated above whether it meets the requirements of a specific program or not needs to be checked against those stated requirements by your company. E-signature information. - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/creating-learning-approval-processes/how-sap-successfactors-learning-uses-e-signatures 
Paul

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM cTMS configuration and SAP Solman integ...

I can highly recommend to directly approach SAP Success Factors via the influence portal: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/86 - You input will help to highlight the demand for this once more! 

 All the best,  Sven

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to dynamically set the basic authentication for t...
entry.content:
Hi Frank!

 

Assuming that the KVM looks like this:

nameOfMyKVM

dynamicUsername*****
dynamicPassword*****

 

You can add a Key Value Map Operations Policy to your API Proxy to access the values:

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="nameOfMyKVM" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Get assignTo="private.usernameFromKVM" index="1">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>dyanmicUsername</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.passwordFromKVM" index="1">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>dynamicPassword</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

(Note that you won't see variables starting with the prefix "private." in the debug mode of the API Proxy.)

 

Now you can add a Basic Authentication Policy to assign the variables "private.usernameFromKVM" and "private.passwordFromKVM" to the Authorization header of your request.

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Encode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='private.usernameFromKVM'></User>
	<Password ref='private.passwordFromKVM'></Password>
 	<AssignTo>request.header.Authorization</AssignTo>
</BasicAuthentication>

entry.title: Re: How to backup transactional data of SAP BPC 10.1 on S...
entry.content: Hello William, Thank you for your suggestion. I have a question regarding the backup process. If I create a backup of data from 2013 to 2015 in one environment, and later wish to create a backup for the period from 2016 to 2017, do I need to establish a new environment for this purpose, or can I utilize the same environment where the 2013-2015 backup is stored?

entry.title: Re: Can somene help me how to deactivate SAP GTS to the P...
entry.content:
Hi,

please check customizing function "Control Data for Transfer to SAP Global Trade Services" (ECC) or "Configure Control Settings for Document Transfer" (S/4HANA).
You can deactivate transfer of Purchase Orders for Compliance Checks on Application Level MM0A for single document types or document type/company code.

Additionally, if you use SAP S/4HANA, you can deactivate function "prevent GR for blocked purchase documents" in this customizing path.

BR
Fabian

entry.title: Re: Email Template - TotalGrossAmount value NOT in availa...
entry.content:
Hallo @MartinHubert 

We are on onPremise and we did the followed the steps below. It's working well in PRD but I have been wondering what could the potential downsides. We used this for sales order and return workflow 

  1. Created a new CDS view (join existing one and add new fields)
  2. Assign the new CDS to the standard template using transaction SMTG_WB_START
  3. Copy the standard template to custom and change the fields in the template with the normal process

I hope this will help.

entry.title: Now Available: Manage Your SAP Business AI Features Flexibly from a Single Place
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that in close collaboration with SAP Business AI, SAP for Me and SAP Central Business Configuration, AI-assisted scoping and configuration services we now offer a tailored view on AI features that matter for you.

You can access this view in SAP for Me under Portfolio & Products and then Business AI. It includes the AI features that are relevant to you, depending on the SAP solutions you’re running. For now, we’re guiding you along the creation of a support case to activate AI features easily and fast. While this is a beta version, you will soon be able to instantly activate or deactivate and test out AI features with the touch of a finger and unleash direct value in seconds.

 

Screenshot of SAP for Me, Product & Portfolio, Business AI

Screenshot of Support Case Creation

This free-of-charge service is a game-changer for you as SAP cloud customers, providing you with the autonomy and flexibility to manage your SAP Business AI features according to your specific business needs centrally.

Stay tuned for more!

entry.title: Re: Inventory audit report and trail balance amount miss...
entry.content: Transaction - Fiori app MB5L - List of Stock Values: Balances can show the detail of the differences. We can select whole Company Code values per previous period and tick on Display Indiv. material. The list will help to identify the material in issue. Investigation of the document of such NOK material will help to find the issue. The issue could be caused by material price update without proper update to GL (i.e. stock transfer between materials which have different price).

entry.title: Re: Inventory audit report and trail balance amount miss...
entry.content: Yes, posting to GL without material document would make miss reconciliation between MM and FI reports. (in FS00 the stock account has NO Auto posting only)

entry.title: Re: Can the internal job posting be restricted to some su...
entry.content:
Hi!

This is a known product behaviour and there is an influence request for it on the influence portal under the name"Internal Job Posting does not restrict any employees from accessing any kind of jobs"

 

I hope this helps,

entry.title: Bring Your Own Identity: SAP's Strategic Transformation of Identity and Authentication Management
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the foundation for Bring Your Own Identity (BYOI) by Identity Renovation, an innovative transformation designed to enhance interactions with SAP's digital platforms. 

Our vision is to enable straightforward digital interactions and processes for customers from their initial contact to known user status, consistent across all digital touchpoints and cloud products. This approach will be implemented without the constraints of scaling, performance, compliance, and security issues inherent in legacy identity management systems.

Customers and partners adopting Bring Your Own Identity will gain complete control over user management, ensuring smooth integration and increased security. They can tailor Identity & Access Management (IAM) setups to their specific needs while ensuring compliance and mitigating risks of data breaches in a cloud-ready environment.

This initiative represents a strategic enhancement designed to improve interactions with SAP's digital platforms by allowing customers and partners to connect their Corporate Identity Providers (IdPs) with SAP. Our development teams are actively working on its implementation to make authentication more efficient across all SAP touchpoints, so stay tuned for updates.

To facilitate this transition, we are optimizing our Identity & Access Management (IAM) systems for seamless registration and integration of Corporate Identity Providers. This will enable customers and partners to use their existing corporate credentials, providing unified access to both corporate applications and the SAP ecosystem.

We are undertaking these comprehensive changes to meet the growing expectations of our customers and partners for a modern, secure, and cohesive digital experience with SAP. This effort is crucial not only to ensure up-to-date security but also to provide smooth user interactions and build trust across our digital platforms. The goal is for customer identities to be onboarded once and utilized across all SAP products and digital services, enabling secure and compliant management of authorizations.

Implementing Bring Your Own Identity is a detailed process. To ensure a smooth transition, we are planning a pilot phase with a selected group of pilot customers starting in Q3 2025. Following the pilot, we aim for a global rollout later in 2026. This phased approach will help us refine and perfect the system to better meet the needs of all our users worldwide.

The target audience for the global rollout includes all customers and partners equipped with their own corporate Identity Provider and prepared to embrace Bring Your Own Identity.

Find more information about the topic on YouTube: How Identity Renovation will Simplify User Authentication at SAP Touchpoints in the Future

Join our Continuous Influence Session to share detailed ideas and requirements on Bring Your Own Identity, S-user Management, and SAP Universal ID, ensuring clarity and effective review by SAP. Please note that an S-user is required to submit improvement requests.

entry.title: Re: SAC connection with SAP on premise HR system
entry.content:
Hi Maria,

Cloud Connector | SAP Help Portal, required(tutorial->link)

Expose CDS views via annotations or create OData services.

In Analytics Cloud->Create a data connection.

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Re: How can I deploy my completed SAP Build Apps web appl...
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/btp-destinations-and-sap-build-apps-to-integrate-sap-c4c-s-4hana/ba-p/13551925?utm_source=chatgpt.com

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: Escaped the House! fun challenge thanks @Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: Re: Font Size in UIBB in sap mdg fpm
entry.content: Hello, to me 'tax numbers' looks like a header line of a table, whereas 'relationships' is a header of a section. This would explain the different sizes.

entry.title: How to map AD group to roles in HANA Cockpit
entry.content:
How the AD group are mapped with the standard roles available in the HANA Cockpit also need to know how to get the details of user to role. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to map AD group to roles in HANA Cockpit
entry.content:
You can refer this guide and find the role mapping in the XSA  How to Configure SAP HANA Cockpit with SAP Cloud Platform Identity Authentication Step-by-Step

You can also check this note for more information 2569903 - How to configure SAML authentication on HANA XSA https://me.sap.com/notes/2569903

entry.title: Receive the click-to-dial event in the CTI widget
entry.content:
Hi team,
SAP service cloud and sales cloud version 2
On the Contacts page, when clicking on any phone number, it attempts to open a new call application and displays the message "Allow https:// to open the tel link".
So I need to disable this default behavior in order to receive the event in my CTI widget script.

entry.title: Re: SAP GTS QUESTION
entry.content:
Hi,

maybe Sales Order document type is not activated for transfer to SAP GTS.

Please check customizing function "Configure Control Settings for Document Transfer" in S/4 system.

Additionally, please check BAdi "Control Interface Calls from Sales Documents (SD0A).

BR
Fabian
